opp.ais_why:
• AI importance is rated 'Very Critical' and the opportunity info notes an "interest in understanding AI enabled capabilities". But it qualifies that "no quantified business case has yet been established" and the opportunity is in "early stage qualification". Products do not include Agentic AI or ANA.
• The March 11 2026 demo call captured a single action item with direct buyer verbatim: Fernando Puerto asked to "Disable the AI note-taking feature for the call to comply with GTA policy". That is a government-policy resistance to AI capture - an important nuance for the AI-comfort signal.
• The most recent update on May 5 records Fernando saying the team no longer has bandwidth to engage in the project. The demo found sourcing was not a fit, and CLM and eProc are shortlisted. The Zycus 'Very Critical' flag is not supported by buyer-voice evidence, and the buyer-side actions (disable AI recording, project pause) push toward the Resistant edge. Confidence is low.

sources: Next_Step__c 11 Feb 2026: intro with Fernando, strong fit and need, high AI interest
Next_Step__c 11 Mar 2026: demo occurred, sourcing not quite the tool they need, but CLM and eProc fan
Avoma demo call 11 Mar 2026
metrics.narrative: Fernando Puerto expressed strong fit and need during the 11 February 2026 intro call, with high AI interest noted by the rep (source: Next_Step__c 11 Feb entry). The 11 March 2026 demo call surfaced that sourcing is not quite the tool they need, but CLM (contract lifecycle management) and eProcurement are a fan (source: Next_Step__c 11 Mar entry and Avoma demo call). No quantified business case, cost savings, cycle time reduction, or ROI has been articulated by the buyer. The qualitative pain is partial: a procurement modernization need with AI appetite, but the sourcing module misfit is a material gap. The buyer has not quantified the impact of their current pain or the value Zycus would deliver, and no Economic Buyer has validated the business case. To strengthen: secure a quantified ROI or savings projection tied to CLM and eProcurement scope, and validate it with the budget authority.

sources: Next_Step__c 11 Feb to 5 May 2026: Fernando Puerto as single point of contact
Next_Step__c 5 May 2026: team no longer has bandwidth, requesting 2-3 month delay
Engagement pulse: 107 days dark, no buyer response since 5 May
champion.narrative: Fernando Puerto (Director, Procurement) is the only engaged buyer contact and is a weak, at-risk champion. He initiated the relationship on 11 February 2026 (intro call), participated in the 11 March 2026 demo, and was responsive to scheduling through mid-March. However, he did not bring the legal team to the planned 24 March 2026 CLM deep dive, did not reply to multiple rep nudges (30 Mar, 7 Apr, 10 Apr, 24 Apr, 29 Apr 2026), and on 5 May 2026 communicated that the team no longer has bandwidth to engage and requested a 2-3 month delay (source: Next_Step__c 5 May entry). Since then, Fernando has been non-responsive (52 days of silence). There is no evidence of advocacy: he has not introduced Zycus to the Economic Buyer, legal team, or procurement committee; he has not pushed back on competitors; and he has not driven the internal agenda. He has no confirmed access to the budget holder or decision maker. His engagement is passive and cooling, not active. To secure or rebuild: the deal owner or the deal owner's manager must re-engage Fernando with a value-driven reason to restart the conversation (e.g., a Merlin CLM use case, an ERP integration roadmap, or a peer reference), and simultaneously multi-thread to the legal team and the budget authority to reduce single-point dependency.

sources: SF Competitors__c: JAGGAER, Others
Next_Step__c 11 Mar 2026: sourcing not the right fit (exposure vs. full-suite competitors)
competition.narrative: JAGGAER is the named primary competitor (source: SF Competitors__c field). An 'Others' competitor is also listed but not specified (source: SF Competitors__c field). No call transcript, Next_Step__c entry, or Task mentions a competitive bake-off, shortlist, or head-to-head comparison, so the competitive threat level and the buyer's sentiment toward JAGGAER are unknown. The sourcing misfit (Zycus sourcing is not the right tool for GTA per the 11 Mar demo) is a material exposure: if JAGGAER offers a stronger sourcing module or a better-fit full suite, Zycus is vulnerable on completeness and may lose to a peer platform that covers all three modules (sourcing, CLM, eProcurement) cohesively. The deal has been dark for 107 days, and the buyer requested a 2-3 month delay on 5 May 2026, so competitive activity during the silence is unobserved. To win: position Zycus as the best-fit CLM and eProcurement solution with AI/Merlin differentiation, and either partner to fill the sourcing gap or reframe the scope to exclude sourcing and focus on the two modules where Zycus is a fan favorite. Re-engage to learn whether JAGGAER or another rival has advanced during the dark period.

sources: Next_Step__c 11 Feb 2026: strong fit and need, high AI interest
Next_Step__c 11 Mar 2026: sourcing not the tool, CLM and eProc fan; ERP switch planned
Avoma demo call 11 Mar 2026
identify_pain.narrative: The confirmed pain is a need to modernize procurement operations with a focus on contract lifecycle management (CLM) and eProcurement, driven by high AI interest (source: Next_Step__c 11 Feb and 11 Mar entries, Avoma demo call 11 Mar 2026). Fernando Puerto (Director, Procurement) is the pain owner. The buyer's current sourcing tool is not being replaced by Zycus; sourcing is not the right fit for their use case (source: Next_Step__c 11 Mar). The inferred pain is that GTA's existing CLM and eProcurement processes are manual, disconnected, or inadequate, and they see AI as a lever to improve efficiency. They are also planning an ERP switch (source: Next_Step__c 11 Mar), which implies broader systems pain and a forcing function for procurement suite integration. However, no quantified pain (cost of current process, cycle time, error rate, compliance risk) has been articulated. The operational or financial impact of not solving this pain is unstated. To deepen: ask Fernando to describe the current-state pain in CLM and eProcurement workflows, quantify the cost or risk of the status quo, and confirm the timeline and trigger for the ERP switch.

sources: Next_Step__c 11 Mar 2026: ERP switch planned, integration discussion needed
Next_Step__c 16 Mar 2026: CLM deep dive with legal team planned but not completed

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics (MSA, SOW, legal review, procurement channel, InfoSec) are unmapped. The 11 March 2026 demo indicated an upcoming deep dive with the legal team for CLM (source: Next_Step__c 16 Mar entry), but that call was never completed. No MSA, Order Form, SOW, or legal redline discussion has occurred. The buyer mentioned a planned ERP switch (source: Next_Step__c 11 Mar), which implies a systems integration scoping requirement, but no formalized integration or implementation plan has been discussed. GTA is a public-sector entity and likely follows a procurement code requiring competitive bidding, board approval, or committee sign-off for six-figure software purchases, but none of that has been documented. No InfoSec questionnaire, privacy review, or procurement channel (direct vs. cooperative contract vs. state purchasing agreement) has been identified. To gain visibility: ask Fernando Puerto to describe GTA's procurement process, legal review steps, and contracting timeline, and request introduction to the legal and InfoSec stakeholders.

sources: Next_Step__c and Avoma manifest: only Fernando Puerto engaged; no EB/CFO/sponsor named

economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer has been identified or engaged in this cycle. Fernando Puerto (Director, Procurement) is the single point of contact; his title suggests operational authority but not budget or commercial decision-making power for a $150k spend. The Next_Step__c and call history show no CFO, finance lead, or C-level sponsor involvement in the 11 Feb intro or 11 Mar demo. The buyer mentioned a planned ERP switch (source: Next_Step__c 11 Mar entry), which implies a larger technology transformation budget, but the budget holder and approval chain for this procurement suite purchase are unmapped. GTA is a public-sector entity, likely requiring procurement board or committee approval for six-figure software buys, yet no such process or sponsor has surfaced. To close this gap: ask Fernando Puerto who holds budget authority for this project and request an introduction to that person or committee, then confirm the approval process and timeline.

sources: Next_Step__c 11 Feb to 5 May 2026: call sequence and rep outreach attempts
No Salesforce Tasks, Events, or field data describing the process
decision_process.narrative: No decision process has been mapped. The call sequence shows an intro call on 11 February 2026 (not recorded) and a demo on 11 March 2026 with Fernando Puerto and unspecified team members. A second demo with the legal team was planned for 24 March 2026 but did not happen (no follow-up logged). The rep attempted to nudge the legal team via Fernando on 30 March 2026, and planned a call for 10 April 2026 to discuss CLM deep dive and eProcurement fitment, but no completed call or outcome is recorded. On 5 May 2026, Fernando communicated that the team no longer has bandwidth to engage and requested a 2-3 month delay (source: Next_Step__c 5 May entry). Since then, the deal has been silent. The approval chain, procurement board involvement, legal/InfoSec review steps, contract negotiation process, and timeline to decision are all unknown. GTA is a public-sector authority, likely requiring multi-stakeholder committee approval, but that process has not been articulated. To map: re-engage Fernando and ask directly for the decision steps, timeline, and approval chain from evaluation to contract execution.

sources: Next_Step__c 11 Mar 2026: sourcing not the tool they need, CLM and eProc fan; ERP switch planned

decision_criteria.narrative: The buyer's evaluation began with a demonstration on 11 March 2026 covering sourcing, CLM, and eProcurement modules. Fernando Puerto and team confirmed that sourcing is not the right fit for their use case, but CLM and eProcurement align well (source: Next_Step__c 11 Mar and Avoma demo call). A follow-up deep dive with the legal team for CLM was planned for 24 March 2026 (source: Next_Step__c 16 Mar entry), but that call did not occur (no Avoma record, no completed task). The buyer is also evaluating integration options because they plan to switch ERPs shortly (source: Next_Step__c 11 Mar entry). No formal RFP, RFI, or written business requirements document (BRD) has been shared. The criteria are inferred: CLM and eProcurement functionality, ERP integration capability, and AI/Merlin features (high AI interest noted). However, the specific decision rubric, weighting, scoring, or formal shortlist steps are undocumented. To confirm: obtain the BRD or evaluation scorecard, and map the decision committee and their priorities.

However, the Next_Step__c entry from 11 February 2026 explicitly states 'high AI interest' during the intro call with Fernando Puerto, and the Products field includes Merlin and Merlin Intake. The buyer is AI Curious at minimum, possibly AI Hungry given the explicit interest and the inclusion of Merlin products in scope.

deal_movement.summary: Created 11 February 2026. Intro call with Fernando Puerto same day (high AI interest, strong fit noted). Demo on 11 March 2026 (sourcing misfit identified, CLM and eProcurement strong interest). Legal team deep dive planned for 24 March 2026 but never occurred. Rep attempted re-engagement 30 March, 7 April, 10 April, 24 April, and 29 April 2026 with no buyer response. On 5 May 2026 Fernando communicated loss of bandwidth and 2-3 month delay request. Deal has been silent for 107 days since then. Last Salesforce activity 11 March 2026.

items.detail: Deal has been dark for 107 days; buyer requested 2-3 month delay on 5 May 2026 and has been non-responsive since. Close date of 22 October 2026 (118 days out) is not credible given the current silence and loss of buyer bandwidth.

Single-threaded to Fernando Puerto (Director, Procurement) who has no confirmed budget authority and has gone silent for 107 days. No Economic Buyer, legal team, procurement committee, or IT/ERP stakeholders engaged. Zero buyer-side contact roles on opportunity. High ghost risk.

Sourcing module misfit: buyer explicitly stated on 11 March 2026 demo that sourcing is 'not quite the tool they need'. This is a material gap if GTA requires a full procurement suite (sourcing + CLM + eProcurement) and exposes Zycus to full-suite competitors like JAGGAER.

No Economic Buyer or budget authority identified; no approval chain or procurement process mapped. Public-sector entity likely requires committee or board approval for $150k spend, but that process is undocumented.

GTA plans to switch ERPs shortly (mentioned 11 March 2026); ERP vendor, timeline, integration scope, and systems requirements are unspecified. Integration capability is a criterion but Zycus position is unconfirmed.
No pricing, commercials, or ROI discussion has occurred. Amount is $150k but no breakdown by module, term, or services. No buyer validation of budget or willingness to pay.

There is no evidence of advocacy, no confirmed access to the Economic Buyer or decision maker, and no multi-threading beyond him. The deal is single-threaded to a cooling, passive contact.

items.expected_effect: Turn the sourcing misfit into a win by positioning a two-module best-fit solution (CLM + eProcurement + Merlin AI) as faster to value and better aligned with GTA's stated needs. Narrow the competitive field and neutralize full-suite peers.

confidence_signals.summary: Deal is cooling and at high risk of being lost or indefinitely delayed. Fernando Puerto requested a 2-3 month delay on 5 May 2026 due to loss of team bandwidth, and the relationship has been non-responsive for 107 days since. No multi-threading, no Economic Buyer, no decision process, no follow-up CLM demo with legal team (planned for 24 March 2026 but never occurred). The sourcing module misfit is unresolved. Engagement is passive and the deal is single-threaded to a contact who has explicitly signaled deprioritization.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 11 Feb 2026; 135 days old. Close date 22 Oct 2026 (118 days out). LastActivityDate 11 Mar 2026 (107 days ago). Time in Formal Evaluation stage: approximately 135 days (no stage-date series available; stage has been Formal Evaluation since creation). Dark for 107 days. Close date is still 4 months away but the deal has been cold for over 3 months, placing it Off Track by the 60+ day cold rule.
evidence: Last buyer touchpoint: demo call 11 Mar 2026 (107 days ago) where Fernando Puerto and team participated; sourcing was not the right fit but CLM and eProcurement showed interest.
Rep attempted re-engagement 30 Mar, 7 Apr, 10 Apr, 24 Apr, and 29 Apr 2026 (voicemails, messages, emails) with no buyer reply logged.
On 5 May 2026 (52 days ago), Fernando Puerto communicated that the team no longer has bandwidth to engage and requested 2-3 month delay.
No verified buyer engagement since 5 May 2026; rep has attempted outreach but the relationship is non-responsive.
Stage is Formal Evaluation but no evaluation activity (POC, deep-dive, legal demo, or contract discussion) has occurred in over 3 months.
Pipeline forecast with $150k amount is not defensible: no Economic Buyer identified, no champion confirmed, no decision process mapped, and the buyer has explicitly communicated loss of bandwidth.
Deal is 107 days cold by the engagement pulse; Off Track by the 60+ day cold rule.
north_star_verdict.headline: Deal remains dark for 107 days since Fernando Puerto requested a 2-3 month delay on 5 May due to lost bandwidth. Multiple re-engagement attempts unanswered. No evaluation activity, no champion, no EB mapped, and sourcing module is not a fit.

best_practice_check.summary: This deal is dead in the water: 107 days dark, single-threaded to a non-responsive contact who explicitly requested a 2-3 month delay 52 days ago, no Economic Buyer or decision process mapped, and a sourcing module misfit that exposes Zycus to full-suite competitors. The highest-leverage action is to re-engage Fernando Puerto with a compelling reason to restart (Merlin CLM use case, ERP integration roadmap, or peer reference), qualify hard on timeline and budget authority, and multi-thread immediately to the legal team and procurement leadership to reduce single-point dependency and map the real decision process.

competitive_position.summary: JAGGAER is the primary named competitor; threat level is medium and status is active by default (listed in Competitors__c but no recent call or activity to confirm current engagement). The sourcing module misfit (Zycus sourcing is not the right tool for GTA per 11 March 2026 demo) is a material exposure: if JAGGAER or another rival offers a cohesive full-suite solution covering sourcing, CLM, and eProcurement, Zycus is vulnerable on completeness. An 'Others' competitor is also listed but unspecified. The deal has been dark for 107 days, so competitive activity during the silence is unobserved. Zycus is well-positioned on CLM and eProcurement with AI/Merlin differentiation, but the sourcing gap and the buyer's loss of bandwidth (5 May 2026) have stalled the deal before a competitive down-select could occur. The single strongest current threat is JAGGAER, a known procurement suite peer, because GTA is likely evaluating full-suite platforms and Zycus has a module gap.

Re-engage to learn JAGGAER's current position and counter with a tailored CLM + eProcurement + Merlin story.

• The March 11 2026 demo call captured a single action item with direct buyer verbatim: Fernando Puerto asked to "Disable the AI note-taking feature for the call to comply with GTA policy". That is a government-policy resistance to AI capture - an important nuance for the AI-comfort signal.
• The most recent update on May 5 records Fernando saying the team no longer has bandwidth to engage in the project. The demo found sourcing was not a fit, and CLM and eProc are shortlisted. The Zycus 'Very Critical' flag is not supported by buyer-voice evidence, and the buyer-side actions (disable AI recording, project pause) push toward the Resistant edge. Confidence is low.

deltas.to: Re-engage a 107-day dark deal with a value-driven reason to restart: the sourcing misfit and bandwidth issue (5 May 2026) have stalled the deal, but CLM and ePr
deltas.subject: Re-engage a 107-day dark deal with a value-driven reason to restart: the sourcing misfit and bandwidth issue (5 May 2026) have stalled the deal, but CLM and eProcurement interest (11 March 2026) and high AI appetite (11 February 2026) are still on record. Lead with a tailored Merlin CLM use case for public-sector contract management, an ERP integration roadmap (GTA plans an ERP switch), or a peer state/local government reference to re-open the conversation and qualify whether the 2-3 month delay is a real pause or a polite brush-off.

deltas.to: Multi-thread immediately beyond Fernando Puerto to reduce ghost risk and map the real decision committee: zero buyer-side contact roles after 4.5 months is a si
deltas.subject: Multi-thread immediately beyond Fernando Puerto to reduce ghost risk and map the real decision committee: zero buyer-side contact roles after 4.5 months is a single-point failure. Ask Fernando to introduce the legal team lead (CLM deep dive was requested but never happened), the IT or ERP program lead (integration is a criterion), and the budget authority or procurement committee chair (public-sector entity likely requires board or committee approval for $150k spend). If Fernando will not facilitate introductions, escalate to a public-sector executive sponsor on the Zycus side to reach GTA leadership directly.

deltas.to: Reframe the product scope to turn the sourcing misfit into a win: Zycus sourcing is not the right fit (11 March 2026 demo), but CLM and eProcurement are fan fav
deltas.subject: Reframe the product scope to turn the sourcing misfit into a win: Zycus sourcing is not the right fit (11 March 2026 demo), but CLM and eProcurement are fan favorites and GTA has high AI interest. Position a two-module solution (CLM + eProcurement + Merlin AI) as the best-fit, fast-to-value play, and either partner to fill the sourcing gap or exclude sourcing from the deal entirely. This narrows the competitive field to CLM/eProcurement specialists and neutralizes full-suite peers like JAGGAER who may be leveraging a complete offering against Zycus.
